name: Let Supervisor recover failed Goals autonomously

notes.author: Codex
notes.body: Parked by user decision pending Feature 00000SZ2HY4R5J9V6AM5XVA00A. Preserve Supervisor recovery candidate aafdc696 as a reviewable prototype. Resume with a new round only after Goal 00000SZ2HZHQX2TXXQ7SXVA00D is reviewed and merged, using the shared consistency and durability contract instead of introducing a Supervisor-specific transaction model.

failed_actions.message: failed_goal_decision implements the Supervisor's adaptable recovery judgment in bespoke Rust using category gates and substring matching. It automatically denies every non-build/quality failure and every governance failure before the installed Supervisor agent evaluates whether a bounded correction is clear.
failed_actions.rule: Keep Refine agent-first: delegate adaptable reasoning and execution to installed agents through shared context and tools instead of duplicating it in bespoke product logic or surface-specific controls. Add first-class functionality when it provides durable coordination, state, synchronization, supervision, evidence, recovery, accessibility, deterministic integrity, or a materially better shared human and agent surface; preserve one capability across UI, CLI, API, and MCP.

failed_actions.message: Recovery checks only the managed-process registry. It does not inspect, close, or replace an existing Claimed or Running WorkflowClaim before changing failed to todo, so workflow claim/capacity state can remain active and prevent or overlap the recovery round.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The tests stop after confirming failed-to-todo mutation and do not execute the recovery round through workflow/Git or cover evidence-write failure. The implementation commits the Goal mutation before writing the required transcript and activity records; if either write fails, the Goal is already todo and later reconciliation skips it, leaving the required shared evidence permanently incomplete.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report:
Implemented Goal `00000SZ2C8SVXH5MGTX1XVA001` and marked it complete.

Verification:

- `cargo test --lib --no-fail-fast` — 400 passed, 0 failed.
- Supervisor recovery tests — 13 passed, covering automatic recovery, governance escalation, live-owner blocking, deduplication, and cycle prevention.
- `cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings` — passed.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — passed.
- `git diff --check` — passed.

Key changes:

- [supervisor_agent.rs](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ2C8SVXH5MGTX1XVA001-round-1/src/tools/product/supervisor_agent.rs:315) now inspects failed-Goal logs, runtime ownership, diagnostics, and reachable Git candidates.
- [service.rs](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ2C8SVXH5MGTX1XVA001-round-1/src/tools/product/work_items/service.rs:1653) atomically appends one structured recovery Round and transitions `failed → todo`.
- Safe build/quality failures recover automatically. Governance, ambiguous evidence, credentials/authority, destructive choices, live workers, missing repository evidence, and failed recovery Rounds escalate without mutation.
- Recovery and escalation evidence is deduplicated in the shared Supervisor transcript and activity log.
- Prompts, workflow context, intent documentation, and runbook now describe the new boundaries.

The worktree contains only the ten scoped modified files and is ready for review. Goal usage: 269,344 tokens over approximately 14m56s.
rounds.prompt: Make Supervisor own routine workflow recovery. It should notice failed Goals, inspect Goal, runtime, and repository evidence, and, when a safe correction is clear under existing authority, append one recovery Round and return the Goal to Todo without user action. Escalate only product judgment, new authority, or destructive choices. Prevent duplicate or cyclic retries, respect workflow and governance boundaries, and record the evidence and action in the shared transcript and activity log. Verify automatic recovery and escalation cases.

failed_actions.message: Supervisor recovery and the separate workflow runner perform unlocked read-modify-write cycles on the entire workflow automation state. The Supervisor lock is not shared by workflow promote/start/complete paths, allowing claim updates to be overwritten or started after recovery's liveness check while the Goal is returned to Todo.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: reconcile_failed_goal_claims does not validate the Goal or claim against the active Node. It interrupts claims and releases capacity before prepare_supervisor_recovery_round_summary performs its ownership check, so recovery of a remote-owned Goal causes claim side effects before returning an ownership conflict.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The claimed exact dossier fingerprint contains only Goal id, round, category, and truncated message—not Goal contents, claims, runtime, repository, operations, or activity. Evidence deduplication also ignores the decision: after recording an escalation, a recover request with the same fingerprint is accepted, its transcript/activity records are suppressed as duplicates, and the Goal becomes Todo while durable evidence still reports escalation without mutation.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report:
Completed Goal `00000SZ2C8SVXH5MGTX1XVA001`. The worktree is clean and reviewable.

Commits:

- `6381598` — preserved Round 1 candidate; stable patch ID matches `ebfeec18...`.
- `5fe8b0d` — Round 2 governance remediation.

What changed:

- The singleton Supervisor agent now receives the full failure dossier and owns recovery judgment; product code only validates and coordinates the decision via [supervisor_agent.rs](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ2C8SVXH5MGTX1XVA001-round-2/src/tools/product/supervisor_agent.rs:235).
- Durable claims and managed processes are reconciled through the existing workflow engine before recovery in [workflow/mod.rs](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ2C8SVXH5MGTX1XVA001-round-2/src/workflow/mod.rs:235).
- Recovery now uses a restart-safe prepare/evidence/complete transaction, preventing an unrecorded Todo transition after evidence-write failure.
- Shared CLI/API adapters, prompts, documentation, and end-to-end Git/workflow failure-path coverage were added.

Deterministic verification:

- `cargo test --all-targets --no-fail-fast` — passed; 403 library tests, 0 failures.
- Supervisor recovery suite — 16 passed.
- Work-item suite — 19 passed.
- Workflow automation suite — 22 passed.
- Prompt suite — 4 passed.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — passed.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — passed.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- Candidate stable patch IDs — identical: `69374303959fe3723b4386abdb95a293532649d7`.
- Final `git status --short --branch` — clean.
rounds.prompt: Preserve candidate ebfeec18a1ad4ed9afd63ad25b252178f954b16f and remediate all three governance findings. Keep recovery agent-first: remove bespoke category gates and substring heuristics from failed_goal_decision, and instead present failed Goal, runtime, repository, claim, operation, and activity evidence to the installed singleton Supervisor agent so adaptable bounded-recovery judgment remains in the agent; product code should provide only deterministic coordination, idempotency, limits, and auditable tools. Before any failed-to-todo transition, reconcile the durable WorkflowClaim and managed process state so a Claimed or Running execution cannot overlap, leak capacity, or be silently replaced; wait/escalate when ownership is live and close only terminal stale claims through the existing workflow engine. Make round append, shared transcript/activity evidence, retry marker, and state transition atomic or restart-safe so any evidence-write failure cannot leave an unrecorded todo Goal, and retries remain duplicate/cycle bounded. Add focused end-to-end workflow/Git recovery coverage plus live-claim, duplicate, escalation, and transcript/activity write-failure tests. Preserve confirmation, destructive-authority, worktree, merge, provider, and authentication boundaries; do not invent a second recovery engine.

failed_actions.message: Cancelling an execution with a live managed process marks its claim Cancelled without releasing its capacity lease. When the worker exits, mark_claim_state rejects its Failed or Completed update because the claim is already terminal, so the release path is never reached. Because the lease holder is the long-lived workflow-runner PID, dead-lease pruning retains it, leaving terminal work consuming capacity until an explicit retry or runner restart.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: Escalation is not restart-safe across Supervisor-session replacement. It writes transcript evidence before activity and Supervisor state but creates no recovery journal. If the activity write fails and that session is subsequently closed, recorded_recovery_decision searches only the new active session plus activity, so it misses the durable escalation in the old transcript and can accept a conflicting recovery for the same dossier.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

failed_actions.message: The claimed full-dossier transaction does not serialize or version the Goal, activity log, operation registry, managed-process records, or repository evidence. submit_recovery_decision fingerprints an earlier Goal snapshot, while prepare_supervisor_recovery_round_summary rereads the Goal but checks only status and Round count. A concurrent Goal edit or evidence append is therefore accepted without recomputing the fingerprint, and the recovery Round can record a fingerprint that no longer describes the authoritative dossier; the concurrency tests cover claim races but not this cross-surface evidence race.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

verdict.message: Three concrete violations remain in cancellation capacity handling and recovery evidence integrity.

violations.message: Cancelling an execution with a live managed process marks its claim Cancelled without releasing its capacity lease. When the worker exits, mark_claim_state rejects its Failed or Completed update because the claim is already terminal, so the release path is never reached. Because the lease holder is the long-lived workflow-runner PID, dead-lease pruning retains it, leaving terminal work consuming capacity until an explicit retry or runner restart.
violations.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

violations.message: Escalation is not restart-safe across Supervisor-session replacement. It writes transcript evidence before activity and Supervisor state but creates no recovery journal. If the activity write fails and that session is subsequently closed, recorded_recovery_decision searches only the new active session plus activity, so it misses the durable escalation in the old transcript and can accept a conflicting recovery for the same dossier.

rounds.implementation_report:
Completed Goal `00000SZ2C8SVXH5MGTX1XVA001`; the worktree is clean and reviewable at commit `2d56ca2`.

Key changes:

- Added one shared, re-entrant workflow/capacity transaction in [transaction.rs](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ2C8SVXH5MGTX1XVA001-round-3/src/workflow/transaction.rs).
- Serialized promote, start, completion, cancellation, retry, capacity, and Supervisor recovery state changes.
- Added Goal and claim Node-ownership preflight before process, claim, or capacity side effects.
- Journaled recovery before closing stale claims; prepared and queued journals repair transcript, activity, Goal, and Supervisor state after crashes.
- Fingerprints now cover the complete canonical dossier without truncation and bind idempotency to the selected action.
- Added start/completion race tests, remote ownership tests, decision-conflict coverage, full-dossier sensitivity, and transcript/activity/Round/state failure recovery.

Verification:

- `cargo test --all-targets --no-fail-fast` — passed; 413/413 library tests, with the xtask wrapper also passing 413/413.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — passed.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — passed.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- Preserved candidate patch IDs — identical: `d387a5f6fc74f4b49704ba1edc04cea1ae978d89`.
- Final `git status --short --branch` — clean.

Goal usage: 297,641 tokens over approximately 26m03s.
rounds.prompt: Preserve candidate 5fe8b0dee3735789a0fb65890215cadec4cd820b and remediate the three remaining governance findings. Introduce one shared workflow-state transaction/lock primitive used by every WorkflowClaim and capacity read-modify-write path—runner promote/start/complete/cancel and Supervisor recovery—so recovery atomically validates liveness, ownership, claim state, capacity, retry marker, and failed-to-todo transition against the same current state; do not add another supervisor-only lock. Validate Goal and claim Node ownership before interrupting a claim, terminating a process, or releasing capacity, and leave remote-owned state untouched with an explicit escalation. Build the recovery dossier fingerprint from canonical full Goal/round failure content plus claim, managed process/runtime, repository/worktree/ref, durable operations, and relevant activity evidence (no truncation); include the requested decision/action in the idempotency identity or reject a different decision for an already-recorded dossier so escalation evidence can never suppress a later mutation record or permit an unrecorded Todo transition. Make transcript/activity/round/state writes restart-safe under that transaction and add concurrency tests racing workflow start/complete with recovery, remote-ownership no-side-effect tests, full-dossier change sensitivity, escalation-then-recover conflict tests, and crash/retry evidence consistency tests.

failed_actions.message: The shared workflow transaction does not cover the Goal, activity, operation, managed-process, or Git writers whose data forms the dossier. Those writers can change evidence after submit_recovery_decision rebuilds the fingerprint but before it journals or projects the decision, so a decision can still be recorded or applied against stale evidence. The tests mutate evidence before submission and do not exercise this in-transaction race.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

failed_actions.message: The canonical repository dossier omits the failed Goal's isolated worktree status and does not resolve its preserved branch ref. Changes to that worktree or branch can therefore leave the fingerprint unchanged, while the repository-staleness test covers only a commit in the primary target repository.

rounds.implementation_report:
Blocked by the Goal’s explicit prerequisite; no files were changed.

- Current candidate remains clean at `aafdc69667ed`, preserving parent `2d56ca2655c6`.
- `main` is `6b9638ce18eb`; dependency `a026e1f` is not merged.
- Goal `00000SZ2HZHQX2TXXQ7SXVA00D` remains actively in progress.
- Its worktree has uncommitted changes in the shared coordination, Git, project-layout, and workflow files.
- No tests were run because implementing now would violate the parking instruction and create a competing consistency model.

Resume this Goal after the shared workflow coordination Goal is reviewed and merged.
rounds.prompt: Preserve candidate 2d56ca2655c64682e10024f0d057fe2e6c50fe7c and fix the three independent governance violations from Round 3. (1) Cancellation with a live managed process must release the exact claim's capacity lease even when the claim is already marked Cancelled before the worker exits; terminal worker updates may be rejected, but cleanup must be idempotent and unconditional so the long-lived workflow-runner PID cannot keep a ghost lease. Add a live-process cancellation regression proving capacity is immediately reusable without retry or runner restart. (2) Make recovery decisions durable across Supervisor session replacement with one authoritative recovery journal keyed by dossier fingerprint/decision, written transactionally before or with transcript/activity/state projections. recorded_recovery_decision must consult this journal, including after the originating session closes, and conflicting decisions must remain rejected after injected activity/state failures and daemon/session replacement. Do not treat transcript as the sole authority. (3) Make dossier submission optimistic-concurrency safe across all authoritative evidence it fingerprints: Goal and round, activity, relevant operations/process records, repository state, and any decision state must carry/recompute a version or canonical fingerprint inside the same locked decision transaction. Before side effects, rebuild the full dossier under the shared workflow-state transaction and reject/retry if it differs; never record a fingerprint for stale evidence. Add deterministic cross-surface race tests that append activity or mutate Goal/operation/repository evidence between dossier creation and submission and prove stale recovery is refused with no partial side effects. Retain single shared workflow-state locking, remote ownership checks, agent-first decision reasoning, and all prior recovery behavior. Run focused concurrency/failure/restart tests, all targets, fmt, clippy, diff checks, and independent governance.
